title: Lemon Curd
excerpt: If you've ever wondered whether you can make lemon curd, then let me tell you, you can! It's delicious on a pavlova mixed with stiffly whipped cream, on scones and as a cake filling Ingredients 4 egg yolks 150 g castor sugar 100 ml fresh lemon juice Zest of one lemon finely grated …
html:
If you've ever wondered whether you can make lemon curd, then let me tell you, you can! It's delicious on a pavlova mixed with stiffly whipped cream, on scones and as a cake filling

Ingredients

  • 4 egg yolks
  • 150 g castor sugar
  • 100 ml fresh lemon juice
  • Zest of one lemon finely grated(zest)
  • 90 g butter
  • Pinch of salt

Method

  1. Put a pot of boiling water on the stove
  2. Set a glass bowl on top of the pot. The bowl must not touch the water. The water must simmer gently
  3. Melt the butter in your glass bowl and take it off the stove
  4. Whisk the rest of the ingredients in with the butter and put your glass bowl back on your pot
  5. Now whisk for about 12 minutes until it starts to thicken. Use a balloon whisk. If you dip a spoon in and draw a line with your finger and it doesn't close up, then your curd is ready
  6. Pour into a jar or bottle. Put it in the fridge
    With the whites you make a meringue
